Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With Previous Court Orders And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-06-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Raymond Burke for plaintiff and moving party Rafael Ramirez

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Rafael Ramirez's motion to compel compliance with prior orders and for monetary sanctions is granted. No opposition has been filed. The Court finds that Defendant failed to comply with the prior orders. Additional monetary sanctions in the amount of $450 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to David Kiernan Replogle, Esq., for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar/fee paid. Proceedings reported by: Ann Mendoza, CSR#6812. (212)

Off Calendar Feb-10-2006 Continued To Jul-12-06 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 306

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Discovery Order;
Off Calendar Jan-27-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David Replogle for defendant and moving party: Pascual Huizar
Michael Heath for plaintiff and opposing party

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with monetary sanctions awarded against defendant in the amount of $450 to be paid on or before February 13, 2006.
Defendant's motion to set aside the previous order to deem certain matters admitted is granted pursuant to CCP Section 2033.300. The Court notes that responses to the requests for admissions were served before the date of the issuance of the order. Counsel for plaintiff shall submit a supplemental declaration indicating the amount of costs incurred in responding to the instant motion so the Court may issue an order pursuant to CCP Section 2033.300(c)(2). COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Responses; To Deem Matters Admitted, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents and to deem certain matters admitted is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objection within 15 days of notice of this order. The matters set forth in plaintiff's first set of requests for admissions are admitted. Failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $336.30 are to be paid within 15 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Comm. Bruce E. Chan.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Responses; To Deem Matters Admitted, And Request For Sanctions
